examples: The wild climate swings shown by GRIP in the last interglacial are not seen in the GISP2 core.
Earth was already in one of its warm interglacial periods when we started burning fossil fuels.
During previous interglacial periods, when glaciers retreated, they left behind open land in their wakes.
In the last interglacial, which lasted thousands of years, Greenland did not shed all of its ice.
Consider that we are currently about 10, 000 years into a typical 12, 000 to 18, 000 year-long interglacial period.
